title: Gas prices keep climbing, and Massachusetts may see some of the biggest hikes
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts gas prices rose 50 cents to $3.40 per gallon due to Iran war disrupting global oil markets.
Diesel prices in Massachusetts increased to $4.83 per gallon, affecting delivery costs and potentially raising other goods' prices.
New England's lack of major gas pipelines and refineries makes the region vulnerable to ongoing global oil price fluctuations.

title: Patriots reportedly agree to 4-year contract with WR Romeo Doubs
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Patriots agree to a four-year contract with WR Romeo Doubs, potentially worth up to $80 million.
Romeo Doubs joins from the Packers after a season with 55 catches, 724 yards, and six touchdowns.
The signing aims to support quarterback Drake Maye following the release of receiver Stefon Diggs.

title: Airbnb is offering $750 to new hosts in World Cup cities, including Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Airbnb offers $750 to new hosts in Boston during FIFA World Cup 2026 to meet demand.
Boston hosts expected to earn an average of $5,200, second only to New York/New Jersey hosts.
More than 380,000 fans anticipated to use Airbnb for World Cup stays, with host city searches up 80%.

title: Wellesley native running the Boston Marathon to honor family with Alzheimer’s
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Colin Rowan, a 53-year-old from Portland, Oregon, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer's Association.
Rowan honors his family members with dementia, including his mother and late grandmother, by supporting the Alzheimer's Association MA/NH Chapter.
Childhood memories of watching the Boston Marathon in Wellesley inspired Rowan to participate and raise awareness for Alzheimer's support.

title: Fundraiser launched for Reddit tipster who helped identify suspect in Brown shooting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A fundraiser has been launched for 'John', the Reddit tipster who helped identify the suspect in the Brown shooting.
The campaign has raised over $10,000 toward a $50,000 goal to aid John, who is currently without permanent housing.
The funds will help cover housing, dental care, student debt, and job-readiness costs for John, an anonymous Brown graduate.

title: Joe Mazzulla praises Baylor Scheierman’s work ethic
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Celtics' Baylor Scheierman praised by coach Joe Mazzulla for his competitiveness after a win against the Cavaliers.
Scheierman scored 16 points and grabbed 10 rebounds in 26 minutes during the Celtics' 109-98 victory.
Despite a thumb fracture, Scheierman has played 59 games, averaging 4.8 points and 3.2 rebounds this season.

title: Police are searching for North Shore suspect in string of indecent assaults
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Revere police are searching for a Hispanic male suspect in multiple indecent assaults near the Beachmont MBTA Station.
The suspect approached female victims from behind between 10 p.m. and 11 p.m., touching them inappropriately before fleeing.
Police are urging locals to check surveillance footage and report suspicious activity; contact Revere Police at 781-286-1212.

title: Wu announces millions in new funding for immigrant services
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu announced a $4.4 million partnership to aid immigrant services in Boston.
The Boston Foundation, Barr Foundation, and United Way committed $3.1 million; the city added $1.3 million.
Funds will support legal aid, mental health services, resource delivery, language learning, and emergency preparedness.

title: Report: Flag football game featuring Tom Brady moved out of Saudi Arabia
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Fanatics Flag Football Classic featuring Tom Brady has been relocated from Riyadh, Saudi Arabia to Los Angeles due to regional tensions.
Tom Brady, Jalen Hurts, and other NFL stars will participate in the game on March 21 at BMO Stadium in Los Angeles.
US military activities and Iranian drone strikes in the Middle East prompted the decision to change the game's location.

title: Tell us: What’s been Joe Mazzulla’s best soundbite?
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla is known for his memorable soundbites during his tenure in Boston.
Boston.com seeks fan input to determine Mazzulla's best soundbite for a March Madness-themed sports bracket.
Fans can submit their favorite Mazzulla quotes based on criteria like humor, profundity, or uniqueness.
